quiz.title: Unit 5 Quiz 10, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Common Names vs Systematic Names
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Common Names of Chemical Compounds, Systematic IUPAC Nomenclature, Everyday Chemicals (Water, Ammonia, Baking Soda, Lime, etc.)

questions.question: Which is the systematic name for water?
questions.explanation: The systematic IUPAC name for water is hydrogen monoxide (or dihydrogen monoxide). While commonly called water, the systematic name reflects its composition of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom.

questions.question: Which is the common name for $\text{H}_2\text{O}$?
questions.explanation: The common name for $\text{H}_2\text{O}$ is water. It is so widely used that the systematic name (hydrogen monoxide) is rarely employed in everyday contexts.

questions.question: Which is the common name for $\text{NH}_3$?
questions.explanation: The common name for $\text{NH}_3$ is ammonia. It is one of the most widely produced industrial chemicals and is used in fertilizers and cleaning products.

questions.question: Which is the systematic name for $\text{NH}_3$?
questions.explanation: The systematic IUPAC name for $\text{NH}_3$ is nitrogen trihydride, though it is almost universally called ammonia. Azane is an alternative systematic name but less common.

questions.question: Which is the common name for $\text{H}_2\text{O}_2$?
questions.explanation: The common name for $\text{H}_2\text{O}_2$ is hydrogen peroxide. It is a common bleaching agent and disinfectant.

questions.question: Which is the common name for $\text{NaHCO}_3$?
questions.explanation: $\text{NaHCO}_3$ is commonly known as baking soda. It is used in cooking as a leavening agent and also in cleaning and antacids.

questions.question: Which is the systematic name for $\text{NaHCO}_3$?
questions.explanation: The systematic IUPAC name for $\text{NaHCO}_3$ is sodium hydrogen carbonate. Sodium bicarbonate is also widely accepted but slightly less systematic.

questions.question: Which is the common name for $\text{CaO}$?
questions.explanation: $\text{CaO}$ is commonly known as quicklime. It is produced by heating limestone and is used in various industrial processes. Lime (A) often refers to calcium hydroxide ($\text{Ca(OH)}_2$).

questions.question: Which is the systematic name for $\text{CaO}$?
questions.explanation: The systematic name for $\text{CaO}$ is calcium oxide. It is an ionic compound consisting of $\text{Ca}^{2+}$ and $\text{O}^{2-}$ ions.

questions.question: Which is the common name for $\text{NaCl}$?
questions.explanation: $\text{NaCl}$ is commonly known as table salt. It is essential for human life and widely used for seasoning and preserving food. Halite (C) and rock salt (D) are also common names but table salt is the most everyday term.

questions.question: $\text{HNO}_3$ is also known as:
questions.explanation: $\text{HNO}_3$ is systematically named trioxonitrate(V) acid. The Roman numeral (V) indicates that nitrogen is in the +5 oxidation state, and 'trioxo' indicates three oxygen atoms bonded to nitrogen. This is the IUPAC systematic name for nitric acid.

questions.question: Which is the systematic name for $\text{NaCl}$?
questions.explanation: The systematic name for $\text{NaCl}$ is sodium chloride. It is an ionic compound formed from sodium ions ($\text{Na}^+$) and chloride ions ($\text{Cl}^-$).

questions.question: Which is the common name for $\text{Na}_2\text{CO}_3$?
questions.explanation: $\text{Na}_2\text{CO}_3$ is commonly known as washing soda. It is used in laundry detergents and water softening. Soda ash (C) is also a common name for the anhydrous form.

questions.question: Which is the common name for $\text{CaCO}_3$?
questions.explanation: $\text{CaCO}_3$ is commonly known as limestone. It is a sedimentary rock composed primarily of calcium carbonate and is used in construction, cement production, and as a building material.

questions.question: Calcium Carbonate is also known as:
questions.explanation: Calcium carbonate ($\text{CaCO}_3$) is systematically named trioxocarbonate(IV) in some nomenclature systems. The (IV) indicates that carbon is in the +4 oxidation state, and 'trioxo' indicates three oxygen atoms bonded to carbon.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered both common and systematic names for everyday chemicals! 🧪📝
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the differences between common names and IUPAC systematic names.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on the most common household chemicals and their formulas.
scoreMessages.0: Please review common names for everyday substances and their corresponding systematic names.
